stem: You are researching your own error-mitigation method and want EstimatorV2 to return raw, unmitigated expectation values (turning off all built-in error mitigation and suppression). Which setting does this?

explanation.correct: Resilience level 0 means 'no mitigation' — it turns off the built-in error mitigation and suppression so the Estimator returns raw expectation values. This is the documented way to disable everything when you plan to apply your own mitigation.

distractors.A: Level 2 is the most aggressive built-in setting (readout mitigation plus ZNE and gate twirling) — the opposite of turning mitigation off.
distractors.B: Level 1 is the default and still applies readout-error mitigation (TREX); it does not give raw, unmitigated values.
distractors.C: Enabling zne_mitigation adds zero-noise extrapolation — that is more mitigation, not less.
